prompt:
Two of us wrote battleship bots for the office tournament and we keep arguing about whose referee is right, so I'm commissioning a neutral one. Plain Java, stdlib only. I want a Board class plus a Match class (Board.java, Match.java or however you organize it).

The grid is 10x10, rows A-J top to bottom, columns 1-10 left to right, coordinates like B7 (case-insensitive on input; uppercase everywhere in output). A fleet layout (see fleets/*.fleet) has one line per ship, '<class> <start> <H|V>' — H extends right, V extends down. The fleet is exactly one carrier (5), battleship (4), cruiser (3), submarine (3) and destroyer (2). Board.parse(String) must reject with IllegalArgumentException: unknown classes, a class placed twice or missing, ships running off the board, overlaps, malformed coordinates (K1, A0, A11, B02, missing fields, bad direction letter) — and our house no-touch rule: ships may not occupy neighbouring squares AT ALL, diagonal contact included.

Board.shoot(coord) returns 'miss', 'hit', or 'sunk <class>' on the shot that takes a ship's last square; shooting any square twice is IllegalArgumentException, shooting once the fleet is destroyed is IllegalStateException. Board.allSunk() says whether the fleet is gone. Two renders, each ten lines 'A ..........' (row letter, space, ten cells, rows joined with newline, no trailing newline): renderOwn() is the defender's view — '#' intact ship square, 'X' hit ship square, 'o' shot-up water, '.' untouched sea; renderTracking() is what the opponent may see — 'X' hits, 'o' misses, '.' everything else.

Match.run(layoutA, layoutB, shotsA, shotsB) referees a full duel between two scripted bots (shot lists as List<String>; fixtures in shots/*.shots, one coordinate per line). A fires first at B's board and turns strictly alternate. Every shot appends a line '<n> <A|B> <coord>: <result>' with n the global shot counter starting at 1. The match ends the instant a fleet is wiped out: append 'winner <player> on shot <n>' and return — the loser gets no reply shot and unused script entries stay unused. Every line newline-terminated. A script running dry before a decision is IllegalStateException; a bot repeating a square lets the board's own error escape.

TestMain.java pins a complete fixture duel between fleets/alpha.fleet and fleets/bravo.fleet using shots/alpha.shots and shots/bravo.shots. 'java TestMain.java' is the acceptance gate; tests and fixture files are read-only.
notes: 7G6 item 3: battleship placement validation + shot resolution + in-process scripted-bot match runner. The full 39-shot match transcript was hand-interleaved from the two fixture shot lists before the reference existed (reference passed 19/19 first run). Pins: no-touch rule incl. diagonal-only contact, OOB/overlap/duplicate/missing/unknown/coord validation (each error fixture checked pairwise so only ONE violation is present), sink announcements, repeat-shot and post-destruction errors, own-vs-tracking renders, strict alternation, match halting on the winning shot with loser unreplied, script-exhaustion error. Domain check: no existing battleship seed in any language; differs from go-ttt-server (network protocol) — this is an engine-level match runner per the wave's multiplayer spread.
